_description: Site-wide index of all notation and symbols across all papers in the series.

notation.name: quaternion algebra
notation.meaning: The Hamilton quaternion algebra: the four-dimensional division algebra over \mathbb{R} with basis {1, i, j, k}.

notation.name: quaternion
notation.meaning: A quaternion q = a + b\mathbf{i} + c\mathbf{j} + d\mathbf{k} with a, b, c, d \in \mathbb{R}.

notation.name: quaternion basis units
notation.meaning: The three imaginary basis units of \mathbb{H} satisfying \mathbf{i}^2 = \mathbf{j}^2 = \mathbf{k}^2 = \mathbf{i}\mathbf{j}\mathbf{k} = -1.

notation.name: quaternion conjugate
notation.meaning: The quaternion conjugate of q = a + b\mathbf{i} + c\mathbf{j} + d\mathbf{k} is \bar{q} = a - b\mathbf{i} - c\mathbf{j} - d\mathbf{k}.

notation.name: quaternion norm
notation.meaning: The norm of a quaternion: |q| = \sqrt{a^2 + b^2 + c^2 + d^2} = \sqrt{q\bar{q}}.

notation.name: unit quaternion group
notation.meaning: The group of unit quaternions: \mathrm{Sp}(1) = \{q \in \mathbb{H} : |q| = 1\}. Isomorphic to SU(2) and topologically homeomorphic to S^3.

notation.name: special unitary group of degree 2
notation.meaning: The Lie group of 2×2 unitary matrices with determinant 1: SU(2) = \{U \in M_{2\times 2}(\mathbb{C}) : U^\dagger U = I, \det(U) = 1\}.

notation.name: special orthogonal group of degree 3
notation.meaning: The Lie group of 3×3 real orthogonal matrices with determinant 1: the rotation group in three dimensions.

notation.name: 3-sphere
notation.meaning: The three-dimensional unit sphere in \mathbb{R}^4: S^3 = \{(a,b,c,d) \in \mathbb{R}^4 : a^2+b^2+c^2+d^2 = 1\}. The underlying topological space of the unit quaternion group.

notation.name: quaternion–SU(2) isomorphism
notation.meaning: The explicit Lie group isomorphism \phi: \mathrm{Sp}(1) \to SU(2) defined by \phi(a+b\mathbf{i}+c\mathbf{j}+d\mathbf{k}) = \begin{pmatrix} a+di & b+ci \\ -b+ci & a-di \end{pmatrix}.

notation.name: double cover homomorphism
notation.meaning: The surjective Lie group homomorphism \pi: \mathrm{Sp}(1) \to SO(3) defined by the adjoint action \pi(q)(v) = qvq^{-1} on pure quaternions, with \ker(\pi) = \{+1, -1\}.

notation.name: unit rotation axis
notation.meaning: A unit vector \hat{n} = (n_x, n_y, n_z) \in S^2 specifying the axis of rotation for a quaternionic gate.

notation.name: rotation angle
notation.meaning: The rotation angle (in radians) for a quaternionic gate. A rotation by \theta about axis \hat{n} corresponds to the unit quaternion q = \cos(\theta/2) + \sin(\theta/2)(n_x\mathbf{i}+n_y\mathbf{j}+n_z\mathbf{k}).
